title: Imaginarity witness
authors: Zhiqi Liang, Yufan Lin, Yu Guo, Yuqi Sun
abstract: Imaginarity has shown to be an important resource in quantum information. The witness theory of quantum resource, such as entanglement witness, coherence witness, and imaginarity witness, has been established, in particular entanglement witness and coherence witness have been extensively explored. We present here another class of imaginarity witnesses beyond the one in [{Phys. Lett. A} \textbf{530}, 130135 (2025)]. Within our framework, any nonreal Hermitian operator under the reference basis is an imaginarity witness and only finite of them can detect all the imaginarity states. As the common approaches that were explored in the witness theories of entanglement and coherence, we then explore the relations between these imaginarity witnesses in different cases: (i) when they can detect common imaginary states, (ii) when they can detect the same sets of imaginary states, and (ii) when they obey the finer relation. Finally, we define an imaginarity measure in terms of witnesses, termed witnessed imaginarity, and prove that it coincides with both the trace norm of imaginarity and the robustness of imaginarity.
summary: This paper introduces 'imaginarity witnesses' as a new tool to quantify the imaginary component of quantum states. It establishes a theoretical framework showing that the resulting 'witnessed imaginarity' measure is mathematically equivalent to both the trace norm and the robustness of imaginarity. This advances the understanding of quantum resource theory by providing a concrete diagnostic tool for imaginary quantum features.
main_problem: The paper develops and analyzes a new class of 'imaginarity witnesses' to characterize and detect imaginary quantum states, extending resource theories like entanglement and coherence.
main_result: The authors prove that a newly defined 'witnessed imaginarity' measure coincides with both the trace norm of imaginarity and the robustness of imaginarity.
method: The methodology involves defining witnesses based on the maximum and minimum eigenvalues of the real part of the witness operator, and then analyzing the geometric relationships between the sets of detectable states.
model_or_system: The study concerns quantum states (density matrices $ho$) acting on a Hilbert space of dimension $d \ge 2$. The analysis uses specific examples like qubits and qutrits.
key_observables: Imaginarity witness ($W$), Imaginarity measure ($	ext{IW}(ho)$), Trace norm of imaginarity ($	ext{Itr}(ho)$), and Robustness of imaginarity ($	ext{IR}(ho)$).
important_parameters: The minimum ($m$) and maximum ($M$) eigenvalues of $	ext{Re}(W)$ define the detection interval $[m, M]$.
main_assumptions: The analysis builds upon the established framework of quantum resource theories and assumes the existence of a reference basis for defining real states.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the concept of imaginarity witnesses, derives new classes of witnesses ($W_{m,M}$), analyzes the relationships between different witnesses (equality and inclusion of detection sets), and culminates in proving the equivalence of the defined imaginarity measure with known quantities.
why_interesting: It provides a rigorous, operational framework for quantifying a specific non-classical feature (imaginarity) of quantum states, drawing direct parallels to well-established concepts like entanglement and coherence witnesses.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
